steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is a transition word that connects two sentences. Sentence 1 states that telling the two raven species apart can confuse even seasoned bird watchers. Sentence 2 gives the reason: the two species share similarities that make them look virtually identical. The transition must signal that sentence 2 explains or confirms sentence 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Instead,': signals contrast or an alternative — but sentence 2 is not a contrast to sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Moreover,': signals addition of a new, separate idea — but sentence 2 does not add a new point; it explains why sentence 1 is true. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Indeed,': signals confirmation or elaboration — sentence 2 confirms why identification is difficult by spelling out the shared similarities. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Thus,': signals a conclusion or result — sentence 2 is the cause of the difficulty, not the result of it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
